Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for PivotPoint relocation details using search_emails (query: "PivotPoint" or "Sam Delgado" or "Nadia Okonjo" or "Diego Castaneda" or similar). Find: (a) pivotpoint_dual_reloc_request from sam@pivotpointlabs.com to catalina.dubois@moveops.com with full details on both moves Nadia Okonjo (SF to Austin, H-1B visa expiring June 15, immigration attorney coordination needed, 5th floor walkup) and Diego Castaneda (Brooklyn to Austin, Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill ~600 lbs/$42K, custom crating, climate-controlled transport, 16-day storage gap). Also, find any follow-up emails from Catalina, coordinators, or vendors about these moves.
OE 2: Search Airtable for both relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Nadia Okonjo") and similarly for Diego Castaneda. Find: (a) recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin (PivotPoint Labs, SF to Austin, In Progress, H-1B visa holder, 5th floor walkup, building super requires 48-hr notice, weekday-only moves), (b) recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin (PivotPoint Labs, Brooklyn to Austin, In Progress, Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill ~600 lbs/$42K, custom vibration-dampened crating, 16-day interim storage gap, climate-controlled with dock access).
OE 3: Search Linear for any existing tracking tickets for Nadia and Diego using linear_list_issues (query: "Nadia" or "PivotPoint" or "Diego" or similar). Determine whether tracking issues exist for each relocation workstream or if they are missing entirely.
OE 4: Search emails for immigration attorney coordination using search_emails (query: "H-1B" or "immigration" or "attorney" or "Lorraine" or "visa" or similar). Determine whether anyone at MoveOps has coordinated with Nadia's immigration attorney about the address change filing timeline relative to the physical move, or if this is still waiting.
OE 5: Search emails for Diego's CNC crating and storage logistics using search_emails (query: "CNC" or "crating" or "storage" or "Diego" or similar). Find any vendor communications about the pre-move inspection window, custom crating arrangements, and interim climate-controlled storage facility (SecureStore Austin or similar).
OE 6: Search Slack for PivotPoint relocation discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "PivotPoint" or "Nadia" or "Diego" or "CNC" or similar). The agent will find PivotPoint mentions related to previous relocations (Ines Kovac, Sasha Greenwald) and general coordinator assignment messages, but no specific messages about the Nadia Okonjo or Diego Castaneda moves. The absence of Slack discussion about these two active relocations is itself a finding it confirms that coordination has not been communicated to the ops team yet.
OE 7: Search CRM for PivotPoint account data using crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint"). Find the company record. Then search crm_search_deals (company_id from above) to find deal_pivot_april_2026_dual ($10,650, in-progress). List engagements using crm_list_engagements to find engagement_pivot_april_2026_dual_reloc (Chloe's ops verification note).
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for PivotPoint financial records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "PivotPoint") or quickbooks_search_customers. Find invoice INV-2026-0315 ($10,650) with 7 line items including: standard relocations, 5th-floor walkup surcharge, custom CNC crating, climate-controlled freight, interim storage, and cargo insurance. Verify the note that says "Non-standard line items #4-7 require ops verification from Chloe Vance before finalizing."
OE 9: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Sam Delgado") to find sam@pivotpointlabs.com for the status update email.
OE 10: If no tracking tickets exist for Nadia and Diego, create Linear issues for each major workstream using linear_create_issue. For Nadia: apartment search in Austin, flight booking SFO to AUS, moving coordination (5th-floor walkup with 48-hr super notice), H-1B visa/immigration attorney coordination. For Diego: apartment search in Austin, flight booking, moving/crating coordination (CNC mill custom crating + pre-move inspection), interim storage setup (climate-controlled with dock access, 16-day gap). Each issue should include the key constraints and deadlines from the relocation details.
OE 11: Send status update email to Sam Delgado using send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, recipients: ["sam@pivotpointlabs.com"]). Content should cover: current status of both relocations, the H-1B attorney coordination status for Nadia, the crating/storage arrangements for Diego, and any items that need Sam's input or PivotPoint's action.
OE 12: Post ops plan in #operations (C006) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload covering: full ops plan for both PivotPoint relocations, coordinator assignments, key constraints (Nadia's H-1B timeline and building access rules, Diego's crating inspection window and storage gap), and action items for each coordinator).
OE 13: Update CRM engagement for PivotPoint using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids with PivotPoint company ID), documenting the current ops status of both relocations and any flagged items from the invoice verification.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 At least four Linear issues were created (via linear_create_issue) for the Nadia Okonjo and Diego Castaneda relocations, covering major workstreams such as H-1B visa coordination, CNC crating/storage, moving coordination, or flight booking.
outcome

5 The email to Sam (visible in the content parameter) states that truck scheduling for Nadia Okonjo's move cannot proceed until written confirmation is received from the immigration attorney that the USCIS address-change filing is handled.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content explicitly states: 'Do NOT schedule truck until attorney confirms filing is handled' and in the ops plan: 'Do NOT schedule truck until attorney confirms filing is handled.' The email to Sam also states: 'We will propose new dates only after we have attorney sign-off on the USCIS filing.' This clearly conveys the truck scheduling hold pending attorney confirmation.
✓Run #2The email content explicitly states: 'Once we have written confirmation from the attorney that the filing is handled (or in process), we will schedule the physical move.' This matches the Airtable constraint 'Do NOT schedule truck until written confirmation from attorney that USCIS filing is handled.'
✓Run #3The email content explicitly states: 'no truck will be scheduled until we have written confirmation that the USCIS filing is handled or in progress.' This clearly communicates the truck scheduling hold tied to attorney confirmation.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly states: 'We will not do so until we have written confirmation from Lorraine that the address-change filing is handled.' This clearly conveys the truck scheduling hold pending attorney confirmation.
✓Run #5The email states: 'Flight and moving truck — These will be booked once we have a confirmed date from the attorney coordination.' This clearly conveys that truck scheduling is blocked on attorney confirmation, satisfying the criterion.
✓Run #6The email content states: 'Do NOT finalize truck date until H-1B attorney coordination is confirmed' and 'apartment sourcing and move scheduling: These are dependent on the attorney coordination being confirmed first... Once we have Lorraine's sign-off on the filing timeline, we'll finalize the truck date.' This captures the operational hold discovered in Airtable.

8 The email to Sam (in the content parameter) includes at least two specific non-standard surcharge line items with their dollar amounts from invoice INV-2026-0315 (e.g., custom crating $1,200, climate-controlled freight $850, interim storage $640, cargo insurance $420).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content references the invoice and mentions 'specialty surcharges (walkup crew, custom crating, climate freight, 16-day interim storage, and cargo insurance rider).' The Slack ops plan explicitly lists all four surcharge items with amounts: custom CNC crating $1,200, climate-controlled freight $850, 16-day interim storage $640, cargo insurance rider $420.
